title: Gluten-Free Fluffy Almond Pancakes
steps: In a large bowl, whisk the eggs, almond milk, maple syrup, and vanilla extract together.
Add the almond flour, baking powder, and salt. Stir until a smooth batter forms.
Heat coconut oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat.
Pour 1/4 cup of batter per pancake. Cook for 3-4 minutes until bubbles start forming on the edges.
Carefully flip and cook the other side for another 2-3 minutes until golden brown.
Serve warm with maple syrup and fresh berries.

substitutions.almond flour: coconut flour (use only 1/2 cup and increase milk to 3/4 cup)
substitutions.maple syrup: honey or monk fruit sweetener (for lower carb/keto)
substitutions.eggs: flax eggs (2 tbsp ground flaxseed mixed with 6 tbsp water)

tips: Keep heat on low-medium. Almond flour burns faster than wheat flour.
Do not make pancakes too large; they are delicate and flip easier when smaller.

title: Vegan Thai Green Curry
ingredients: 2 tbsp Thai green curry paste (ensure vegan/no shrimp paste)
steps: Heat vegetable oil in a large wok over medium heat. Add the green curry paste and sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Slowly pour in 1/2 of the coconut milk while stirring to combine with the paste. Let it simmer for 3 minutes until oil separates.
Add tofu cubes, bell peppers, bamboo shoots, and snap peas. Stir to coat in curry sauce.
Pour in the remaining coconut milk and kaffir lime leaves. Bring to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes until veggies are crisp-tender.
Stir in the soy sauce, coconut sugar, and fresh Thai basil. Cook for another 1-2 minutes.
Remove from heat and serve hot alongside fragrant Jasmine rice.

substitutions.soy sauce: tamari (for gluten-free vegan options) or coconut aminos

tips: Tear kaffir lime leaves slightly before adding to release their aromatic oils.
Do not boil the curry rapidly; gentle simmering keeps the coconut milk from separating.

title: Keto Garlic Butter Beef Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1 lb flank steak, thinly sliced across the grain
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Season sliced beef with salt, black pepper, and 1 tbsp soy sauce.
Melt 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp sesame oil in a large skillet over high heat.
Add beef in a single layer and sear for 2 minutes on each side until browned. Transfer beef to a plate.
Add remaining 2 tbsp butter to the same skillet over medium heat. Sauté the garlic and red pepper flakes for 30 seconds.
Add broccoli florets and remaining soy sauce. Cover and cook for 3 minutes until broccoli is vibrant green and slightly tender.
Return beef and accumulated juices to the skillet. Toss everything together for 1 minute.
Garnish with sesame seeds and serve immediately.

substitutions.flank steak: chicken breast, pork loin, or firm tofu
substitutions.broccoli: asparagus, green beans, or zucchini slices
substitutions.soy sauce: coconut aminos (to lower sodium and keep strict keto)

tips: Thinly slicing flank steak across the grain ensures a tender, melt-in-the-mouth texture.
Ensure skillet is smoking hot before adding beef to get a beautiful restaurant-style sear.

title: Classic Italian Margherita Pizza
ingredients: 1/2 cup canned San Marzano crushed tomatoes
4 oz fresh mozzarella cheese, sliced or torn
steps: Place a pizza stone or baking steel in the oven and preheat to 500°F (260°C) for at least 30 minutes.
Stretch the pizza dough gently on a lightly floured surface into a 12-inch circle.
Dust a pizza peel with semolina flour and place the stretched dough on top.
Spread crushed tomatoes evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border. Season tomatoes with a pinch of salt.
Distribute the fresh mozzarella cheese evenly across the pizza.
Carefully slide the pizza onto the hot stone/steel in the oven. Bake for 8-10 minutes until crust is charred and cheese is bubbly.
Remove from oven. Immediately scatter fresh basil leaves, drizzle with olive oil, slice, and serve.

substitutions.fresh mozzarella: vegan mozzarella (for dairy-free or vegan adaptation)
substitutions.pizza dough: cauliflower pizza crust (for a gluten-free/keto adaptation)

tips: Pat the fresh mozzarella dry with paper towels to prevent the pizza from getting soggy during baking.
Adding basil *after* baking ensures the delicate leaves remain vibrant and don't turn bitter.
